description: General biology covering cell biology, genetics, ecology, and evolution.

facts.context: Mitochondrial DNA inheritance follows the pattern of:
facts.truth: exclusive maternal lineage transmission without paternal contribution
distractors: biparental inheritance with equal contribution from both parents
exclusive paternal lineage transmission without maternal input
random selection of either maternal or paternal mitochondria

facts.context: Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium requires these five conditions:
facts.truth: no selection, no mutation, no migration, random mating, and large population size
distractors: strong selection, high mutation, no migration, random mating, large population
no selection, no mutation, no migration, assortative mating, small population
no selection, no mutation, active migration, random mating, large population

facts.context: The Shannon diversity index H is calculated as:
facts.truth: H = -sum(pi x ln(pi)); higher H indicates greater community diversity
distractors: H = sum(pi x ln(pi)); higher H indicates lower community diversity
H = -sum(pi squared); higher H indicates greater species richness
H = sum(1/pi x ln(pi)); higher H indicates greater evenness only

facts.truth: synthesis proceeds 5' to 3'; the lagging strand uses Okazaki fragments
distractors: synthesis proceeds 3' to 5'; the lagging strand uses Okazaki fragments
synthesis proceeds 5' to 3'; both strands replicate continuously
synthesis proceeds 3' to 5'; the leading strand uses Okazaki fragments

facts.truth: adding TTAGGG hexanucleotide repeats to human chromosome termini
distractors: adding AATCCC hexanucleotide repeats to human chromosome termini
removing damaged nucleotides from human chromosome terminal ends
fusing adjacent chromosome ends through TTAGGG repeat deletion

facts.context: The Krebs cycle yields per glucose molecule entering as two acetyl-CoA:
facts.truth: 2 ATP (via GTP), 6 NADH, and 2 FADH2 per glucose molecule
distractors: 4 ATP (via GTP), 2 NADH, and 6 FADH2 per glucose molecule
2 ATP (via GTP), 8 NADH, and 0 FADH2 per glucose molecule
0 ATP directly, 4 NADH, and 4 FADH2 per glucose molecule

facts.context: Chargaff's rule for double-stranded DNA states:
facts.truth: adenine equals thymine and guanine equals cytosine (base pair complementarity)
distractors: adenine equals guanine and thymine equals cytosine (purine-pyrimidine rule)
adenine equals cytosine and guanine equals thymine (cross-pairing rule)
all four bases exist in equal proportions of 25% each in all DNA

facts.truth: rapid reproduction, small body size, and short lifespan (bacteria, insects)
distractors: slow reproduction, large body size, and long lifespan (elephants, whales)
rapid reproduction, large body size, and long lifespan (primates)
slow reproduction, small body size, and short lifespan (amphibians)

facts.truth: slow reproduction, large body size, and long lifespan (elephants, whales)
distractors: rapid reproduction, small body size, and short lifespan (bacteria, insects)
slow reproduction, small body size, and short lifespan (nematodes)
rapid reproduction, large body size, and long lifespan (sea turtles)

facts.truth: mitochondria and chloroplasts originated as free-living prokaryotes engulfed by host cells
distractors: the nucleus originated as a free-living prokaryote engulfed by a primitive cell
ribosomes originated as free-living prokaryotes incorporated into eukaryotic cells
mitochondria evolved de novo from eukaryotic membrane invaginations without symbiosis

facts.context: The competitive exclusion principle (Gause's law) states:
facts.truth: two species occupying the same niche cannot coexist indefinitely in one habitat
distractors: two species in the same habitat always evolve to share identical niches
competition between species increases biodiversity within the same niche
two species can occupy the same niche indefinitely through mutualism

facts.truth: genetic drift in small colonizing populations reduces genetic diversity from the source
distractors: natural selection in large populations increases genetic diversity over time
gene flow between adjacent populations eliminates all genetic differentiation
mutation pressure in isolated populations always increases genetic diversity
